differentials.name: SEROUS / VISCERAL PERICARDIUM = serosa =  epicardium =  delicate inner visceral layer forming a completely closed sac around the pericardial cavity; consists of a single layer of mesothelial cells

differentials.name: Transverse sinus [Superior (aortic) recess] [Left pulmonic recess] [Right pulmonic recess] [Inferior aortic recess]
can directly influence atherogenesis by free diffusion of fatty acids + adipokines into wall of coronary arteries
differentials.raw: can directly influence atherogenesis by free diffusion of fatty acids + adipokines into wall of coronary arteries

context: =  double-walled sac (2-layered membrane) that envelops all cardiac chambers + origin of great vessels without direct attachment to heart Pericardial thickness:  0.4-1.0 mm (1.2-1.7 mm by MRI; 0.7-2.0 mm by CT) Function:   regulates normal ventricular compliance by minimizing dilatation of RV + RA; physically protects heart by production of fluid + surfactants; limits displacement of heart within mediastinum; acts as pressure transducer between pleural space + cardiac chambers (augments systemic venous return + RV filling during inspiration) √  attaches to sternum + proximal great vessels √  i
